Combine per-layer read results into a single sf, surfacing read failures

Source: R/elsar_load_data.R
combine_layers.Rd

Aggregates the results of several filter_sf() calls (one per file or layer). NULL entries are failed reads; successful reads are sf objects (possibly with zero rows). Unlike a bare dplyr::bind_rows(), this:

Usage

combine_layers(results, sources, noun = "layer")

Arguments

results

List returned from filter_sf() calls; NULL = failed read.

sources

Character vector of the file paths / layer names read, in the same order as results, used in messages.

noun

Character. "file" or "layer", for messages.

Value

An sf object combining all successful reads (possibly zero rows).

Details

  • errors if every read failed (rather than returning a 0 x 0 tibble that is not an sf object), and

  • warns, but continues, if some reads failed, naming which.

On this page

Developed by Scott Atkinson, Sandra Neubert.
